challenge_points.description: All nine citations collapse to just two sources (EIA chokepoint reports and one Statista chart) with no independent verification or primary Iranian data.
Claim that 'no alternative route can absorb Hormuz-level volumes' rests on EIA baselines that pre-date 2022-2024 Saudi/UAE pipeline capacity additions.
Analysis omits Iran's ability to sustain partial or intermittent closure (e.g., mining/threats) rather than full blockade, which alters endurance calculations.

first_pass_summary: The research sessions established the Strait of Hormuz's critical importance to global oil transit but did not directly investigate the core hypothesis about Iran's capacity to endure a prolonged closure. The three sessions consistently surfaced the same limited set of sources—primarily EIA data on chokepoints, Statista visualizations of oil shipments, and general reporting on regional conflict. These sources confirm the Strait's role as the world's most critical oil chokepoint but do not addres…
